summary: AIR-DROW Input, Language & Hand Startup Edition: instant mobile keyboard focus, language-safe runtime check surfaces, honest hand-engine startup guidance, and a calm saved-calibration success transition.
features: Makes real text inputs focus on the first Android tap while preserving selection protection on non-editable AIR-DROW surfaces.
Stores Camera & Hand Check detail keys instead of stale translated strings, so an open check repaints immediately when Kurdish or English changes.
Starts same-origin model/runtime prefetch only after Camera is explicitly chosen, overlaps it with camera allocation, and tells the person to wait before asking them to show a hand.
Shows a saved-calibration success message long enough to read, then closes through a calm exit animation.
Refines light-mode premium palettes and icon contrast without introducing fake device, camera, or hand status.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you choose to export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: App readiness and Hand drawing check only read local browser and app state. They do not open the camera themselves or send a report anywhere.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and hand model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The complete source package includes the verified hand model. The production build validates the local model before publishing.
